findings: PASS: leaf-node lock is explicit and the worker tool list omits task/ask_user (agents/stampede-agent.agent.md:7-14,27-28).

GAP: bash remains available without an explicit ban on using it to invoke other agents/orchestrators, so the leaf rule is policy-only rather than mechanically enforced (agents/stampede-agent.agent.md:8,27-28).

GAP: task-loop guidance claims queue items atomically but does not explicitly forbid writing new queue manifests, leaving a recursion avenue if prompts go off-rail (agents/stampede-agent.agent.md:19-21,23-36,109-148).
GAP: telemetry spawns an untracked background curl process, which is a bounded-execution footgun for a leaf node (agents/stampede-agent.agent.md:332-338).
GAP: queue-empty shutdown is inconsistent with the later idle-retry loop (agents/stampede-agent.agent.md:148,257-258).
